mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55 percent during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by more than 100 basis points over 60 days, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, generating positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or loses predictive power during recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy falling below 52 percent over consecutive 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns exceed 65 percent during periods of rising CPI, or the correlation between CPI growth and equity returns turns positive over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.5 percent confirms robust economic expansion, boosting equity valuations through higher earnings multiples and generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces negative average returns over 60 days in more than 55 percent of triggered cases, or fails to beat a zero-cost benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable federal funds rates indicates a soft landing environment where labor market strength supports earnings without triggering aggressive rate hikes, yielding positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k while FEDFUNDS remains flat, or the combined signal underperforms single-proxy models by more than 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansion phases signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, leading to negative short-term equity returns as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, with signal accuracy dropping below 48 percent over a 36-month evaluation period.
